name: Bangkok
sample_activities: Boat ride on Chao Phraya River
highlights: Bangkok dazzles with ornate temples, legendary street food, and a nightlife scene that never sleeps. The city perfectly blends ancient traditions with modern energy, all at incredibly low prices.

name: Hanoi
highlights: Hanoi captivates with its chaotic charm, from the buzzing Old Quarter to serene lakes and pagodas. The street food here is among the best in the world, with pho and banh mi on every corner.

name: Marrakech
highlights: Marrakech is a sensory overload of colour, spice, and sound. The medina's labyrinthine souks, stunning riads, and the unforgettable Jemaa el-Fnaa square make it one of Africa's most iconic destinations.

name: Budapest
highlights: Budapest is the pearl of the Danube, offering grand architecture, legendary thermal baths, and a thriving ruin bar scene. The city delivers a high-quality European experience at remarkably affordable prices.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Lisbon
highlights: Lisbon enchants with its golden light, pastel-coloured buildings, and soulful fado music. The city's hilly streets reveal stunning viewpoints, world-class seafood, and a creative energy that makes it one of Europe's coolest capitals.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Bali
highlights: Bali is the ultimate tropical paradise, offering lush rice terraces, sacred temples, world-class surf breaks, and a wellness culture unlike anywhere else. From budget hostels to luxury villas, it caters to every traveller.

name: Mexico City
highlights: Mexico City is a sprawling metropolis bursting with culture, from world-class museums and ancient ruins to some of the best street food on the planet. Its Roma and Condesa neighbourhoods are vibrant hubs of creativity.

name: Kraków
highlights: Kraków is Poland's cultural jewel, with a beautifully preserved medieval centre, a moving history, and one of Europe's best nightlife scenes. The Wieliczka Salt Mine and Wawel Castle are unforgettable experiences.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Colombo
highlights: Sri Lanka packs an incredible amount of diversity into a compact island: pristine beaches, lush hill country, ancient ruins, and abundant wildlife. The scenic train rides through tea country are among the world's best.

name: Athens
highlights: Athens is the cradle of Western civilisation, where ancient ruins stand alongside vibrant modern neighbourhoods. The Acropolis dominates the skyline, while below, the lively Plaka district serves exceptional Greek cuisine.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Barcelona
highlights: Barcelona is a feast for the senses, with Gaudí's fantastical architecture, golden Mediterranean beaches, and a food scene that rivals any city in Europe. The energy of La Rambla and the Gothic Quarter is infectious.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Rome
highlights: Rome is an open-air museum where every corner reveals layers of history, from the Colosseum to Renaissance masterpieces. The food alone is worth the trip, with perfect pasta and gelato at every turn.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Prague
highlights: Prague is a fairy-tale city of Gothic spires, baroque palaces, and cobblestone lanes. It boasts some of Europe's finest beer culture and an Old Town that looks virtually unchanged since the Middle Ages.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Istanbul
highlights: Istanbul straddles two continents and thousands of years of history, from Byzantine mosaics to Ottoman mosques. The Bosphorus views, bustling bazaars, and incredible Turkish cuisine make it utterly unique.

name: San José
highlights: Costa Rica is a biodiversity hotspot offering rainforests, volcanoes, and pristine coastlines. It is a world leader in eco-tourism, with over 25% of its land protected as national parks and reserves.

name: Tokyo
highlights: Tokyo is a mesmerising blend of ultra-modern technology and ancient tradition. From Michelin-starred sushi counters to serene Shinto shrines, the world's largest metropolis never ceases to amaze.

name: New York City
highlights: New York City is the city that never sleeps, offering world-class museums, Broadway, and a food scene representing every cuisine on earth. Its iconic skyline and diverse neighbourhoods make every visit unforgettable.

name: Amsterdam
highlights: Amsterdam charms with its elegant canals, world-renowned museums, and famously liberal spirit. Cycling along the canal ring in spring, with tulips in bloom, is one of Europe's most iconic experiences.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Reykjavík
highlights: Reykjavík is the gateway to Iceland's otherworldly landscapes of geysers, glaciers, and volcanic terrain. The midnight sun in summer and Northern Lights in winter offer truly magical natural experiences.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU/EEA freedom of movement)

name: Cape Town
highlights: Cape Town is stunningly situated between Table Mountain and two oceans, offering incredible natural beauty. World-class wine estates, vibrant food markets, and diverse wildlife make it one of Africa's most compelling cities.

name: Dubai
highlights: Dubai is a futuristic city of superlatives: the tallest building, the largest mall, and man-made islands visible from space. It offers luxury shopping, stunning beaches, and thrilling desert adventures.

visa_requirements.IN: Visa on arrival (14 days) or apply online

name: Malé
highlights: The Maldives is the ultimate tropical luxury destination, with crystal-clear turquoise waters and pristine white-sand beaches. Overwater villas and world-class diving make it a dream honeymoon destination.

name: Paris
highlights: Paris is the world's most visited city for good reason, offering unmatched art, fashion, cuisine, and romance. From the Eiffel Tower to hidden bistros, the City of Light delivers magic around every corner.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Interlaken
highlights: Interlaken sits between two glacial lakes with the Swiss Alps as a dramatic backdrop. It is the adventure capital of Europe, offering world-class skiing, hiking, and paragliding amidst breathtaking Alpine scenery.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU/EFTA freedom of movement)

name: Santorini
highlights: Santorini is a volcanic island of whitewashed villages perched on dramatic cliffs above a deep blue caldera. The sunsets in Oia are legendary, and the local wines and seafood are exceptional.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Sydney
highlights: Sydney offers a spectacular harbour, iconic architecture, and golden beaches just minutes from the city centre. The outdoor lifestyle, multicultural dining scene, and natural beauty make it one of the world's most liveable cities.

name: Cancún
highlights: Cancún combines Caribbean beaches with ancient Mayan history. The turquoise waters, cenote swimming holes, and nearby ruins of Chichén Itzá create an unbeatable mix of relaxation and adventure.

name: Orlando
highlights: Orlando is the theme park capital of the world, home to Walt Disney World, Universal Studios, and SeaWorld. It offers endless family entertainment and is a dream destination for children and the young at heart.

name: Queenstown
sample_activities: Jet boat ride on Shotover River
highlights: Queenstown is New Zealand's adventure capital, set against a dramatic backdrop of mountains and lakes. From bungee jumping to jet boating, and from skiing to hiking, it is an adrenaline junkie's paradise.

name: Zanzibar City
highlights: Zanzibar is a tropical archipelago with a rich Swahili trading history, reflected in Stone Town's winding alleys and ornate carved doors. The turquoise waters and white-sand beaches are among Africa's finest.

name: Berlin
highlights: Berlin is Europe's creative capital, where cutting-edge art, legendary nightlife, and a turbulent history converge. The city's affordable cost of living has attracted artists and innovators from around the world.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Vienna
highlights: Vienna is the former seat of the Habsburg Empire, dripping with imperial grandeur, world-class music, and legendary coffeehouse culture. Repeatedly voted the world's most liveable city, it exudes elegance at every turn.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Dubrovnik
sample_activities: Game of Thrones filming locations tour
highlights: Dubrovnik's perfectly preserved medieval walls enclose a stunning old town of marble streets and baroque churches overlooking the Adriatic. Made famous by Game of Thrones, it is one of the Mediterranean's most photogenic cities.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Edinburgh
sample_activities: Visit the National Museum of Scotland
highlights: Edinburgh is a dramatic city of volcanic hills, medieval castles, and Georgian elegance. The annual Edinburgh Fringe Festival is the world's largest arts festival, transforming the city every August.

name: Florence
highlights: Florence is the birthplace of the Renaissance, housing more masterpieces per square metre than anywhere on earth. The Uffizi, the Duomo, and the Tuscan countryside nearby make it an art and food lover's paradise.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Copenhagen
highlights: Copenhagen is the hygge capital of the world, blending Scandinavian design, New Nordic cuisine, and a progressive cycling culture. Home to Noma and a thriving food scene, it consistently ranks among the world's happiest cities.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Stockholm
highlights: Stockholm is built across 14 islands connected by bridges, offering a unique blend of urban sophistication and natural beauty. The medieval Gamla Stan, world-class museums, and archipelago make it Scandinavia's most photogenic capital.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Seville
highlights: Seville is the passionate heart of Andalusia, famous for flamenco, tapas, and stunning Moorish architecture. The Alcázar and the cathedral are among Spain's most impressive monuments, and the spring festivals are legendary.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Porto
sample_activities: Port wine tasting in Vila Nova de Gaia
highlights: Porto is a hilly riverside city of incredible charm, famous for its port wine cellars, blue-tiled buildings, and the dramatic Douro Valley nearby. It offers all the culture of Lisbon at even lower prices.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Bruges
sample_activities: Basilica of the Holy Blood visit
highlights: Bruges is a perfectly preserved medieval city of winding canals, cobblestone streets, and Gothic architecture. Famous for its chocolate, beer, and waffles, it is one of Europe's most romantic small cities.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Munich
highlights: Munich blends Bavarian tradition with cosmopolitan flair, offering beer gardens, world-class museums, and proximity to the Alps. Oktoberfest draws millions annually, and nearby Neuschwanstein is the fairy-tale castle of dreams.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Tallinn
highlights: Tallinn boasts one of Europe's best-preserved medieval old towns, a UNESCO World Heritage site of turrets, spires, and cobblestones. As one of the world's most digital societies, it also has a thriving tech and startup scene.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Bucharest
highlights: Bucharest is an eclectic mix of grand Belle Époque architecture, communist-era monuments, and a booming nightlife scene. Day trips to Transylvania's castles and the Carpathian Mountains add to its appeal.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Sofia
highlights: Sofia is one of Europe's most affordable capitals, set against the backdrop of Vitosha Mountain. It has a layered history spanning Roman, Ottoman, and Soviet eras, all visible in its eclectic architecture.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Valletta
highlights: Valletta is the smallest EU capital, yet it packs in 7,000 years of history, from megalithic temples to Baroque churches. Malta's crystal-clear waters, year-round sunshine, and English-speaking locals make it hugely welcoming.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Seoul
highlights: Seoul is a high-energy megacity where ancient palaces sit next to gleaming skyscrapers. It is the epicentre of K-pop, Korean cuisine, and cutting-edge technology, with incredible street food and nightlife.

visa_requirements.CN: Visa-free for 15 days (transit) or visa required

name: Kuala Lumpur
highlights: Kuala Lumpur is a melting pot of Malay, Chinese, and Indian cultures, reflected in its incredible street food and stunning temples. The Petronas Towers are iconic, and the city offers luxury at budget-friendly prices.

visa_requirements.IN: E-visa or visa on arrival (30 days)

name: Chiang Mai
highlights: Chiang Mai is northern Thailand's cultural capital, surrounded by misty mountains and hundreds of ornate Buddhist temples. It is a haven for digital nomads, with amazing food, affordable living, and a relaxed pace of life.

name: Siem Reap
highlights: Siem Reap is the gateway to Angkor Wat, the world's largest religious monument and one of humanity's greatest architectural achievements. Beyond the temples, the town offers vibrant nightlife and authentic Cambodian cuisine.

name: Kathmandu
highlights: Kathmandu is the gateway to the Himalayas and a spiritual crossroads of Hindu and Buddhist traditions. Ancient temples, prayer flags, and the promise of Everest make it irresistible for adventurers and spiritual seekers.

name: Goa
highlights: Goa is India's beach paradise, blending golden sands with Portuguese colonial heritage. From lively North Goa beach parties to serene South Goa retreats, it offers relaxation and adventure at incredibly low costs.

name: Jaipur
highlights: Jaipur, the Pink City, is a riot of colour with its rose-tinted palaces, bustling bazaars, and mighty hilltop forts. As one corner of India's Golden Triangle, it offers a deeply immersive introduction to Rajasthani culture.

name: Kyoto
highlights: Kyoto is Japan's cultural heart, home to over 2,000 temples and shrines, traditional geisha districts, and stunning seasonal landscapes. Cherry blossoms in spring and fiery maple leaves in autumn are world-famous.

name: Taipei
highlights: Taipei is a vibrant, safe city famous for its incredible night markets, hot springs, and welcoming locals. It offers a unique blend of Chinese, Japanese, and indigenous cultural influences at very reasonable prices.

name: Ho Chi Minh City
highlights: Ho Chi Minh City is a frenetic, motorcycle-filled metropolis with a fascinating war history and some of the world's best street food. The energy is intoxicating, and the Mekong Delta is right on the doorstep.

name: Luang Prabang
highlights: Luang Prabang is a UNESCO-listed gem where saffron-robed monks process at dawn past gilded temples along the Mekong River. The serene beauty, turquoise waterfalls, and unhurried pace make it Southeast Asia's most tranquil town.

name: Phnom Penh
highlights: Phnom Penh is a rapidly developing capital with a poignant history, ornate royal palace, and an emerging food and cocktail scene. The city's riverside location and French colonial architecture give it a unique charm.

name: Lima
highlights: Lima is the gastronomic capital of South America, home to three of the world's top 50 restaurants. The city blends pre-Columbian ruins, colonial architecture, and a vibrant coastal food scene unlike any other.

name: Bogotá
highlights: Bogotá is a high-altitude capital bursting with energy, from its world-class Gold Museum to its vibrant street art scene. Colombia's transformation has made it one of South America's most exciting emerging destinations.

name: Buenos Aires
highlights: Buenos Aires is the Paris of South America, with elegant boulevards, passionate tango, and some of the world's best steak. The vibrant neighbourhoods of San Telmo and Palermo pulse with creativity and nightlife.

name: Havana
highlights: Havana is a time capsule of 1950s Americana, colonial Spanish architecture, and revolutionary history. Classic cars cruise past crumbling pastel buildings while live salsa spills from every doorway.

name: Cartagena
highlights: Cartagena is a Caribbean jewel of colourful colonial architecture, bougainvillea-draped balconies, and buzzing plazas. The UNESCO-listed old city is among the most photogenic in the Americas.

name: Rio de Janeiro
highlights: Rio de Janeiro is one of the world's most dramatically beautiful cities, set between lush mountains and golden beaches. Carnival, samba rhythms, and the iconic Christ the Redeemer statue define its infectious energy.

name: Medellín
sample_activities: Cable car ride to Parque Arví
highlights: Medellín has transformed from its troubled past into one of the world's most innovative cities. Known as the City of Eternal Spring for its perfect climate, it offers vibrant culture, stunning street art, and warm locals.

name: Tulum
highlights: Tulum offers a unique blend of Mayan clifftop ruins, turquoise Caribbean waters, and a boho-chic wellness scene. The nearby cenotes and biosphere reserve make it a paradise for eco-conscious travellers.

name: Vancouver
highlights: Vancouver is consistently ranked among the world's most liveable cities, nestled between the Pacific Ocean and snow-capped mountains. The combination of urban sophistication and immediate access to wilderness is unmatched.

name: Montreal
highlights: Montreal is North America's most European city, where French and English cultures merge into a creative metropolis. It boasts incredible food, world-class festivals, and a joie de vivre that defines Québécois culture.

name: Nairobi
highlights: Nairobi is the only capital city in the world with a national park within its borders, where lions roam against a skyline backdrop. It is the gateway to the Masai Mara and Kenya's incredible wildlife experiences.

name: Cairo
highlights: Cairo is home to the last surviving Ancient Wonder of the World. The Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx, and the Egyptian Museum hold treasures spanning 5,000 years of civilisation that continue to awe visitors.

name: Fez
highlights: Fez is home to the world's largest car-free urban area, a medieval medina that has changed little in a thousand years. The ancient tanneries, ornate madrasas, and labyrinthine souks offer the most authentic Moroccan experience.

name: Essaouira
highlights: Essaouira is a laid-back coastal town with Atlantic winds perfect for surfing, a beautiful blue-and-white medina, and a thriving arts scene. It offers Morocco's relaxed seaside vibe at very affordable prices.

name: Victoria Falls
sample_activities: Bungee jump off Victoria Falls Bridge
highlights: Victoria Falls is one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World, a curtain of water over 1.7 km wide plunging into a gorge. The spray can be seen from 50 km away, earning it the name 'The Smoke That Thunders'.

name: Addis Ababa
highlights: Addis Ababa is the diplomatic capital of Africa and home to 'Lucy', the 3.2-million-year-old human ancestor. Ethiopian cuisine and the traditional coffee ceremony are unique cultural experiences found nowhere else.

name: Accra
highlights: Accra is a rapidly growing West African capital with a vibrant music scene, bustling markets, and a significant role in the history of the Atlantic slave trade. The 'Year of Return' initiative has made it a key diaspora destination.

name: Dakar
highlights: Dakar is a vibrant Atlantic-facing city with a thriving music and art scene, beautiful beaches, and the historically significant Gorée Island. Senegalese hospitality, known as 'teranga', makes visitors feel genuinely welcome.

name: Muscat
highlights: Muscat is a beautifully understated Arabian city with dramatic mountain and coastal scenery. Oman offers the culture and luxury of the Gulf states without the crowds, with stunning wadis and deserts to explore.

visa_requirements.GB: Visa on arrival (14 days, free) or e-visa
visa_requirements.US: Visa on arrival (14 days, free) or e-visa
visa_requirements.EU: Visa on arrival (14 days, free) or e-visa
visa_requirements.AU: Visa on arrival (14 days, free) or e-visa

name: Amman
highlights: Amman is the gateway to Jordan's extraordinary treasures: the rose-red city of Petra, the Dead Sea, and the Martian landscapes of Wadi Rum. The city itself offers excellent Arabic cuisine and genuine hospitality.

visa_requirements.GB: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.US: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.EU: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.AU: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)

name: Tbilisi
highlights: Tbilisi is an emerging gem where ancient churches cling to cliffsides above sulphur baths, and the wine culture dates back 8,000 years. Georgia's incredible cuisine, warm hospitality, and low prices make it a rising star.

name: Baku
highlights: Baku is where medieval caravanserais meet futuristic Flame Towers on the Caspian Sea. This oil-rich capital is rapidly modernising while preserving its UNESCO-listed Old City and unique Zoroastrian fire-worshipping heritage.

name: Auckland
highlights: Auckland is the City of Sails, sprawling across volcanic hills and two harbours. With Waiheke Island's vineyards, nearby Waitomo Caves, and easy access to the rest of New Zealand, it is an ideal starting point for exploration.

name: Nadi
highlights: Fiji is an archipelago of 333 tropical islands with crystal-clear lagoons, world-class diving, and famously friendly locals. The Fijian concept of 'Bula' encapsulates the warmth and joy visitors experience from the moment they arrive.

name: Melbourne
highlights: Melbourne is Australia's cultural capital, renowned for its laneways filled with street art and hidden bars, world-class coffee culture, and diverse food scene. The Great Ocean Road nearby is one of the world's most scenic drives.

name: Singapore
sample_activities: Gardens by the Bay light show
highlights: Singapore is a spotlessly clean city-state that punches far above its weight in cuisine, architecture, and green spaces. Its hawker centres serve some of the world's best street food, and Gardens by the Bay is truly futuristic.

visa_requirements.IN: Visa required (apply online or at embassy)

name: Hong Kong
highlights: Hong Kong is a dazzling collision of East and West, with a spectacular harbour skyline, world-renowned dim sum, and hiking trails minutes from skyscrapers. The energy of this compact city is absolutely electric.

name: London
highlights: London is a global capital of culture, history, and entertainment. From Buckingham Palace to Borough Market, and from world-class free museums to legendary theatres, it offers something for every traveller.

name: Petra
sample_activities: Walk through the Siq to the Treasury
highlights: Petra is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World, an ancient Nabataean city carved into rose-red sandstone cliffs. Walking through the narrow Siq canyon to reveal the Treasury is one of travel's most iconic moments.

visa_requirements.GB: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.US: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.EU: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)
visa_requirements.AU: Visa on arrival (30 days, free with Jordan Pass)

name: Cusco
highlights: Cusco was the capital of the Inca Empire and is the gateway to Machu Picchu, one of the most awe-inspiring ancient sites on earth. The city's blend of Inca walls and colonial churches creates a unique cultural tapestry.

name: Arusha
sample_activities: Hot air balloon over the Serengeti
highlights: Arusha is the safari capital of East Africa, gateway to the Serengeti and Ngorongoro Crater. The Great Migration of over a million wildebeest is one of nature's most extraordinary spectacles.

name: Phuket
highlights: Phuket is Thailand's largest island, offering stunning beaches, crystal-clear waters, and a lively nightlife scene. The nearby Phi Phi Islands and James Bond Island are among Southeast Asia's most iconic coastal scenery.

name: New Delhi
highlights: New Delhi is a sensory overload of ancient monuments, bustling bazaars, and incredible street food. From Mughal-era architecture to the chaos of Old Delhi, it offers one of the most intense and rewarding travel experiences in Asia.

name: Los Angeles
highlights: Los Angeles is the entertainment capital of the world, home to Hollywood, world-class museums, and iconic beaches. The diverse neighbourhoods, from Silver Lake to Venice Beach, each have their own unique culture and cuisine.

name: Zürich
highlights: Zürich is Switzerland's largest city, combining cosmopolitan culture with stunning lakeside and Alpine scenery. Its Old Town, world-class dining, and proximity to ski resorts make it a year-round luxury destination.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU/EFTA freedom of movement)

name: Monaco
highlights: Monaco is the playground of the ultra-wealthy, a tiny city-state of superyachts, grand casinos, and Formula 1 glamour. Despite its size, it packs in incredible Mediterranean views and world-class entertainment.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Doha
highlights: Doha has transformed into a world-class cultural destination, anchored by the stunning Museum of Islamic Art and the traditional Souq Waqif. Qatar's blend of Arabian heritage and futuristic ambition is genuinely impressive.

name: Merzouga
sample_activities: Camel trek into Erg Chebbi dunes
highlights: Merzouga is the gateway to Morocco's Sahara Desert, where towering orange dunes of Erg Chebbi rise up to 150 metres. Sleeping under a canopy of stars in the silent desert is a profoundly moving experience.

name: Hakone
sample_activities: Open-air hot spring with Mount Fuji view
highlights: Hakone is a mountain resort town famous for its hot springs, Lake Ashi views, and proximity to Mount Fuji. Traditional ryokan stays with onsen baths make it the quintessential Japanese relaxation experience.

name: San Francisco
highlights: San Francisco is one of America's most beautiful cities, draped over steep hills with iconic cable cars, the Golden Gate Bridge, and a legendary food scene. Its unique fog, diverse neighbourhoods, and tech culture set it apart.

name: Amalfi
highlights: The Amalfi Coast is a UNESCO-listed stretch of dramatic coastline where pastel-coloured villages cling to sheer cliffs above the Mediterranean. The combination of Italian cuisine, coastal beauty, and la dolce vita is irresistible.

visa_requirements.EU: No visa required (EU freedom of movement)

name: Airlie Beach
highlights: The Whitsunday Islands are 74 tropical islands in the heart of the Great Barrier Reef. Whitehaven Beach, with its pure silica sand and turquoise waters, is regularly voted one of the world's most beautiful beaches.

name: Abu Dhabi
highlights: Abu Dhabi is a gleaming capital of culture and luxury, home to the stunning Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque and the Louvre Abu Dhabi. It offers a more refined alternative to Dubai, with world-class museums and pristine beaches.

visa_requirements.IN: Visa on arrival (14 days) or apply online

name: Ghent
sample_activities: Cruise the medieval canals at sunset on a wooden flat-bottomed boat
Sample three unique lambic beers at Gruut Brewery
Climb the 91-metre Belfry for panoramic views of the Flemish rooftops
Wander through the Patershol neighbourhood's cobbled alleys and hidden bistros
Admire the Van Eyck brothers' Ghent Altarpiece at St Bavo's Cathedral
highlights: Ghent is the Belgian city locals wish tourists would stop overlooking — a car-free medieval centre threaded with canals, packed with student energy and some of the country's best food. Unlike Bruges, it feels like a living city rather than a museum piece, with excellent craft beer bars and a thriving street art scene.

name: Kotor
sample_activities: Hike the 1,350 steps of the fortress walls above the old town at dawn
Kayak across the Bay of Kotor to the tiny island church of Our Lady of the Rocks
Eat fresh black risotto at a family-run konoba on the waterfront
Drive the serpentine road up to Lovcen National Park for fjord views
Explore the quiet village of Perast before the cruise ships arrive
highlights: Kotor's walled old town sits wedged between dramatic mountains and Europe's southernmost fjord, offering Dubrovnik-level beauty at a fraction of the price and crowds. Visit in September when the summer rush fades and locals reclaim the stone piazzas for evening promenades.

name: Mostar
sample_activities: Watch local divers leap 24 metres from the Stari Most bridge into the Neretva River
Sip Bosnian coffee slowly in the traditional way at a riverside cafe in Kujundziluk
Visit the moving War Photo Exhibition to understand the city's recent history
Hike to the Kravice Waterfalls for a swim in turquoise pools
Browse the copper-working shops in the old bazaar where artisans still hammer by hand
highlights: Mostar is a city defined by its iconic arched bridge and the resilience of the people who rebuilt it — a place where Ottoman and Mediterranean cultures collide over strong coffee and river-cooled breezes. The old town is touristy by day, but stay for the evening when locals emerge and the real magic begins.

name: Sintra
sample_activities: Wander the fantasy-coloured turrets of Pena Palace at opening time to avoid crowds
Descend the spiral initiation well at Quinta da Regaleira
Hike the forested trails from Sintra to the Moorish Castle ruins
Try travesseiros — the town's signature pastry — at Piriquita bakery
Catch the train from Lisbon and explore the misty hillside gardens on foot
highlights: Sintra feels like stepping into a 19th-century Romantic painting — misty hilltop palaces draped in forest, connected by mossy paths that wind through fern-filled ravines. Arrive early or late in the day to experience the fairy-tale atmosphere without the Lisbon day-trip crowds.

name: Colmar
sample_activities: Stroll through Petite Venise's half-timbered houses reflected in canal water
Taste five Alsatian wines on a winstub crawl through the old town
Visit the Unterlinden Museum to see the Isenheim Altarpiece
Drive the Route des Vins through vineyards and fairy-tale villages
Feast on tarte flambee and choucroute at a traditional winstub
highlights: Colmar is the Alsatian gem that supposedly inspired the village in Beauty and the Beast — pastel half-timbered houses lining canals, with the driest microclimate in France keeping rain at bay. The wine culture here is deeply local, with families pouring Gewurztraminer in cellars their grandparents built.

name: Hallstatt
sample_activities: Take the funicular up to the world's oldest salt mine and slide down the miners' slides
Kayak on the mirror-still lake at dawn before the tour buses arrive
Hike the Echern Valley trail to Waldbachstrub waterfall
Visit the Beinhaus bone house with its painted skulls in the hillside chapel
Swim in the crystal-clear lake from the tiny public beach near the market square
highlights: Hallstatt is the lakeside Alpine village that launched a thousand Instagram posts — but arrive before 9am or after 5pm and you'll have the pastel houses and mirror-lake reflections largely to yourself. The 7,000-year-old salt mining heritage is genuinely fascinating and far more interesting than just the views.

name: Rovinj
sample_activities: Watch sunset from the rocks below the Church of St Euphemia with a bottle of local Malvazija wine
Swim off the rocky coves in the Zlatni Rt forest park
Browse the open-air art studios in Grisia Street during the summer festival
Eat fresh grilled squid at a harbourside konoba
Cycle the coastal path through pine forests to the Lim Fjord
highlights: Rovinj is Istria's crown jewel — a pastel-coloured fishing village tumbling down a peninsula into the Adriatic, where Italian and Croatian cultures blend seamlessly. The truffle-and-olive-oil food scene rivals Tuscany at half the price, and the sunsets from the church hill are unforgettable.

name: Hoi An
sample_activities: Take a cooking class at a riverside farm using ingredients you pick from the garden
Get a custom suit or dress made in 24 hours at one of the old town tailor shops
Cycle through rice paddies to the beach at An Bang
Release a paper lantern onto the Thu Bon River during the full-moon festival
Eat cao lau noodles — a dish found nowhere else in Vietnam — at the central market
highlights: Hoi An is a lantern-lit ancient trading port where you can eat the best food in Vietnam for under two pounds a dish, get world-class tailoring done overnight, and cycle to an empty beach in fifteen minutes. The full-moon lantern festival turns the old town into something genuinely magical.

name: Penang
sample_activities: Eat your way through Gurney Drive hawker centre — char kway teow, assam laksa, and cendol
Follow the street art trail through George Town's UNESCO heritage zone
Visit the Clan Jetties where Chinese families have lived on stilts over the water for generations
Hike through the canopy walkway at Penang National Park
Take a funicular to Penang Hill for sunset views over the Strait of Malacca
highlights: Penang is Southeast Asia's undisputed street food capital — a place where Malay, Chinese, Indian, and Peranakan cuisines collide in hawker centres that stay packed until midnight. George Town's crumbling colonial shophouses are being reborn as boutique hotels and galleries without losing their soul.

name: Yogyakarta
sample_activities: Watch sunrise over Borobudur temple from the upper terraces
Attend a shadow puppet (wayang kulit) performance at the Sultan's Palace
Explore the hidden Hindu temples of Prambanan at golden hour
Learn batik fabric painting from a master artisan in a home workshop
Ride a motorbike through the emerald rice terraces and small villages south of the city
highlights: Yogyakarta is the cultural soul of Java — a sultan's city where ancient temples, contemporary art galleries, and some of the cheapest street food on Earth coexist in creative chaos. This is where Indonesians themselves go to connect with their heritage, not just a tourist stop.

name: Hampi
sample_activities: Rent a bicycle and explore the 500-year-old Vijayanagara ruins scattered across boulder-strewn landscape
Cross the Tungabhadra River by coracle boat to the quieter hippie side
Watch sunset from Matanga Hill overlooking the entire temple valley
Boulder-hop between ancient carved temples in the royal enclosure
Eat a banana-leaf thali at one of the family-run guesthouses near the bazaar
highlights: Hampi is an open-air museum of the Vijayanagara Empire scattered across a surreal landscape of giant boulders and banana plantations — like an ancient civilisation built inside a Salvador Dali painting. It's one of India's most awe-inspiring sites and remains blissfully uncrowded compared to the northern tourist circuit.

name: Lake District
sample_activities: Hike to the summit of Helvellyn via the knife-edge Striding Edge ridge
Take a vintage steam boat cruise across Windermere at dusk
Visit Beatrix Potter's Hill Top farm in Near Sawrey
Wild swim in Buttermere with fell views in every direction
Eat sticky toffee pudding at a flagstone-floored pub in Ambleside
highlights: The Lake District is England's most dramatic landscape — sixteen lakes and England's highest peaks packed into a compact national park that inspired Wordsworth and Beatrix Potter. Come in October for golden larches, misty mornings, and far fewer hikers on the fells.

name: Bath
sample_activities: Soak in the rooftop pool at Thermae Bath Spa with views over the Abbey
Tour the remarkably preserved Roman Baths by torchlight in the evening
Walk the Royal Crescent and admire Georgian architecture at its finest
Browse the independent shops and cafes along Walcot Street
Take a canal boat trip along the Kennet and Avon Canal to the Dundas Aqueduct
highlights: Bath is a honey-stoned Georgian jewel built around natural hot springs the Romans discovered two thousand years ago — and you can still bathe in them today on a rooftop overlooking the medieval abbey. The city punches well above its weight for independent restaurants, bookshops, and weekend markets.

name: Cotswolds
sample_activities: Walk the Cotswold Way between honey-stone villages and rolling sheep pastures
Browse the antique shops and Friday market in Stow-on-the-Wold
Visit the lavender fields near Snowshill in late June
Have a cream tea in a centuries-old tearoom in Castle Combe
Explore the model village in Bourton-on-the-Water with children
highlights: The Cotswolds is quintessential England distilled into rolling hills, dry-stone walls, and villages built from golden limestone that glows in afternoon light. It rewards slow travel — rent a cottage, walk between pubs, and settle into the rhythm of a countryside that hasn't fundamentally changed in centuries.

name: Scottish Highlands
sample_activities: Drive the North Coast 500 route along dramatic single-track roads
Hike Ben Nevis or the more scenic CMD Arete route to the UK's highest point
Tour a small-batch whisky distillery on Speyside and taste from the cask
Spot red deer, golden eagles, and seals on the Ardnamurchan Peninsula
Wild camp beside a remote loch under the Milky Way
highlights: The Scottish Highlands are Europe's last great wilderness — a landscape of brooding mountains, empty glens, and lochs so still they mirror the sky. The midges are real, but so is the sense of space and solitude you won't find anywhere else in Britain.

name: Cornwall
sample_activities: Surf the Atlantic rollers at Fistral Beach in Newquay
Walk a section of the South West Coast Path between hidden coves
Eat a proper Cornish pasty from a harbour-front bakery in Padstow
Visit the Minack Theatre — an open-air amphitheatre carved into a clifftop
Explore the subtropical gardens of the Lost Gardens of Heligan
highlights: Cornwall is where England meets the Atlantic — rugged cliffs, turquoise coves that rival the Mediterranean on a good day, and fishing villages where the catch goes straight from boat to plate. The food scene has quietly become one of the best in the UK, driven by chefs who fish their own crab.

name: Cappadocia
sample_activities: Float over fairy chimneys in a hot-air balloon at sunrise
Stay in a cave hotel carved into volcanic rock in Goreme
Hike the Rose Valley at sunset through pink-hued rock formations
Explore the underground city of Derinkuyu — eight stories deep
Join a pottery workshop in Avanos using traditional Hittite techniques
highlights: Cappadocia is a geological fever dream — a landscape of fairy chimneys, cave churches, and underground cities carved from volcanic tuff over millennia. Watching a hundred hot-air balloons rise over the valleys at dawn from a cave hotel terrace is one of those rare experiences that genuinely lives up to the hype.

name: Musandam Peninsula
sample_activities: Cruise the Musandam fjords on a traditional dhow boat with dolphins swimming alongside
Snorkel in crystal-clear waters off Telegraph Island
Drive the hair-raising mountain road to Jebel Harim for views of the Arabian and Omani coasts
Camp overnight on a remote beach accessible only by boat
Visit the tiny fishing village of Kumzar, reachable only by sea
highlights: The Musandam Peninsula is Oman's best-kept secret — dramatic fjords slicing through arid mountains, with dolphins in the water and barely a tourist in sight. Often called the Norway of Arabia, it feels like the edge of the world and is only a two-hour drive from Dubai.

name: Stone Town
sample_activities: Wander the labyrinthine alleyways of the UNESCO-listed old town
Eat Zanzibari pizza and sugarcane juice at the Forodhani night food market
Take a spice farm tour and taste fresh vanilla, clove, and cinnamon straight from the tree
Visit the former slave market and underground chambers at the Anglican Cathedral
Sail to Prison Island on a dhow to see giant Aldabra tortoises
highlights: Stone Town is a crumbling, atmospheric maze of carved doorways, rooftop terraces, and spice-scented alleyways where Swahili, Arab, Indian, and European influences have layered up over centuries. The Forodhani Gardens night market at sunset — smoke rising, seafood sizzling — is one of Africa's great sensory experiences.

name: Lamu Island
sample_activities: Sail on a traditional dhow to Manda Island for a deserted beach picnic
Get lost in the car-free alleyways of Lamu Old Town — Kenya's oldest living settlement
Watch boat builders construct dhows by hand using centuries-old techniques
Join a Swahili cooking class to learn coconut fish curry and chapati
Visit the Lamu Donkey Sanctuary and learn about the island's only transport
highlights: Lamu is a Swahili island frozen in time — no cars, no rush, just donkey-wide alleyways between coral stone houses with intricately carved wooden doors. It's the Kenya that existed before the safari lodges, and spending a few days here feels like slipping into a gentler century.

name: Toubkal & High Atlas
sample_activities: Summit Jebel Toubkal — North Africa's highest peak at 4,167m — over two days
Stay in a Berber village guesthouse and eat home-cooked tagine by candlelight
Hike through the Imlil Valley past walnut groves and terraced fields
Visit the weekly Berber souk in Asni where Atlas communities trade
Scramble the Tazaghart Plateau for views that stretch to the Sahara
highlights: The High Atlas is a world away from the medina chaos of Marrakech, just ninety minutes south — Berber villages clinging to mountainsides, terraced orchards, and North Africa's highest peak accessible to anyone reasonably fit. Staying with local families in village guesthouses is the highlight, not just the hiking.

name: Valparaiso
sample_activities: Ride the century-old ascensores (funiculars) between the hilltop cerros
Follow the Open Air Museum street art trail through Cerro Polanco and Cerro Alegre
Visit La Sebastiana — Pablo Neruda's hillside house with harbour views
Eat chorrillana — Chile's ultimate sharing dish of chips, onions, eggs and steak — at J Cruz
Watch the sunset over the Pacific from Paseo 21 de Mayo
highlights: Valparaiso is Chile's gritty, colourful port city tumbling down 42 hills to the Pacific — a place where every wall is a canvas, every stairway a gallery, and the bohemian spirit of Neruda still lingers in the sea air. It's Santiago's creative, rebellious counterpart and far more interesting to explore on foot.

name: Oaxaca
sample_activities: Take a mezcal tasting tour at a family-run palenque in the surrounding villages
Eat tlayudas, mole negro, and chapulines at the Mercado 20 de Noviembre
Witness the Day of the Dead celebrations and comparsas parades in late October
Visit the ancient Zapotec ruins of Monte Alban at sunrise
Learn traditional black clay pottery at a workshop in San Bartolo Coyotepec
highlights: Oaxaca is widely considered the gastronomic capital of Mexico — a place where seven varieties of mole, artisanal mezcal, and indigenous culinary traditions converge in a colonial city draped in street art and creative energy. The Day of the Dead celebrations here are among the most authentic in the country.

name: Sucre
sample_activities: Stroll through the whitewashed colonial centre — Bolivia's most beautiful city
Visit the dinosaur footprints at Cal Orck'o — a near-vertical wall of 5,000 tracks
Browse the Tarabuco Sunday textile market for indigenous weaving
Take a chocolate-making workshop using Bolivian cacao
Hike to the Crater de Maragua — a village inside a meteorite crater
highlights: Sucre is Bolivia's constitutional capital and its most elegant city — a cascade of whitewashed colonial buildings at a pleasant 2,800m altitude with year-round spring weather. While most travellers rush to La Paz and the salt flats, Sucre rewards those who slow down with some of South America's best-value food and genuinely welcoming locals.

name: Banos
sample_activities: Swing over the edge of the world on the swing at Casa del Arbol with volcano views
Cycle the Ruta de las Cascadas — a downhill ride past dozens of waterfalls
Soak in the volcanic hot springs at Termas de la Virgen at night
Bridge-jump (puenting) off the San Francisco bridge into the gorge
Hike to the Pailon del Diablo waterfall and climb behind the cascade
highlights: Banos is Ecuador's adventure capital — a tiny town nestled between an active volcano and the Amazon basin where you can swing over a cloud-filled canyon, cycle past waterfalls, and soak in volcanic hot springs all in one day. The year-round spring climate and rock-bottom prices make it a backpacker's paradise that never feels overrun.

name: Luang Namtha
sample_activities: Trek through the Nam Ha National Protected Area staying in ethnic minority villages
Kayak the Nam Ha River through pristine jungle
Visit a Khmu or Akha hill-tribe village and learn about traditional weaving
Cycle through rice paddies and rubber plantations on quiet rural roads
Cook sticky rice and laap with a local family in their stilted wooden house
highlights: Luang Namtha is the gateway to Laos' most authentic trekking experiences — multi-day hikes through dense jungle to stay with Akha, Khmu, and Tai Dam communities who welcome visitors into their stilted homes. This is Southeast Asia before the crowds found it, raw and real.

name: Nusa Penida
sample_activities: Snorkel with manta rays at Manta Point
Hike down the cliff to Kelingking Beach — the iconic T-Rex-shaped coastline
Dive at Crystal Bay to spot the elusive oceanic sunfish (mola mola)
Swim in the natural infinity pool at Angel's Billabong
Ride a scooter along the rugged west coast roads with ocean views
highlights: Nusa Penida is Bali's wilder, less developed neighbour — dramatic sea cliffs, manta ray encounters, and beaches you have to earn by hiking down steep paths. The roads are rough and the infrastructure basic, but that's exactly what keeps it feeling like the Bali of twenty years ago.

name: Ella
sample_activities: Take the famous blue train from Kandy to Ella through tea country — one of the world's most scenic rail journeys
Hike to Ella Rock at sunrise for misty valley views
Walk across the Nine Arches Bridge when the train passes through
Visit a tea factory and pick leaves with the workers on the plantation
Cook Sri Lankan rice and curry with a local family using garden spices
highlights: Ella is a laid-back hill-country village draped in tea plantations and mist, best reached by the legendary blue train that winds through tunnels, across bridges, and past waterfalls for seven hours. The hiking is gorgeous, the curries are fiery, and the pace of life is exactly what you came to Asia to find.

name: Plovdiv
sample_activities: Explore the 2nd-century Roman amphitheatre still used for concerts in the old town
Wander the Kapana creative quarter's galleries, street art, and craft bars
Visit the painted houses of the Revival-era old town on Nebet Tepe hill
Join the Friday evening wine bar crawl along Otets Paisiy Street
Day-trip to the Bachkovo Monastery and hike the Rhodope mountain trails
highlights: Plovdiv is Europe's oldest continuously inhabited city — older than Rome, Athens, or Constantinople — yet barely registers on most travellers' radars. The old town's painted Revival houses, Roman ruins you can touch, and a buzzing creative quarter make it Bulgaria's cultural capital and one of the continent's best-value destinations.

name: Vis Island
sample_activities: Boat to the Blue Cave on Bisevo island for its ethereal glowing light
Swim at Stiniva Cove — voted Europe's best beach — accessible only by boat or cliff path
Taste indigenous Vugava white wine at a family vineyard on the island
Eat fresh lobster peka at a konoba in the fishing village of Komiza
Explore the abandoned Yugoslav military tunnels and submarine base
highlights: Vis was a closed Yugoslav military base until 1989, which accidentally preserved it as Croatia's most unspoiled inhabited island — no high-rise hotels, no party boats, just vineyards, fishing villages, and the clearest water in the Adriatic. It's where Croatians go on holiday when they want to escape tourism.

name: Matera
sample_activities: Stay in a converted cave dwelling (sassi) hotel carved into the canyon wall
Walk through the Sassi di Matera — the ancient cave city that was once Italy's shame, now its pride
Visit the rock-hewn churches with their faded Byzantine frescoes
Watch sunset from the Belvedere viewpoint opposite the canyon
Eat orecchiette with turnip tops and crusty Matera bread — the city's staple meal
highlights: Matera's cave city is one of the most extraordinary urban landscapes on Earth — thousands of dwellings carved into a limestone canyon, inhabited continuously for 9,000 years. Once called the shame of Italy and evacuated in the 1950s, the sassi have been reborn as boutique hotels, restaurants, and galleries.

name: Granada
sample_activities: Visit the Alhambra at the last entry slot and stay for sunset over the Sierra Nevada
Catch raw, unscripted flamenco in a Sacromonte cave venue
Enjoy Granada's legendary free tapas culture — order a drink, get a plate of food
Wander the Albaicin's narrow Moorish streets to the Mirador de San Nicolas
Soak in an Arab hammam bath house beneath the Alhambra walls
highlights: Granada is where Moorish Spain lives on most vividly — the Alhambra palace floating above the city with the Sierra Nevada behind it, cave flamenco shows in the old Roma quarter, and a tapas culture so generous that a round of drinks comes with free plates of food. It's Spain at its most soulful.

name: Ljubljana
sample_activities: Stroll the car-free city centre along the willow-lined Ljubljanica River
Ride the funicular to Ljubljana Castle for panoramic views
Browse the Friday Open Kitchen street food market on the riverbanks
Day-trip to Lake Bled and walk around the lake to the cliff-top castle
Explore Joze Plecnik's unique architectural vision throughout the city
highlights: Ljubljana is Europe's greenest capital — a pocket-sized, car-free old town straddling a river, topped by a hilltop castle, and surrounded by forests. It has the charm of Prague with none of the crowds, and serves as the perfect base for Slovenia's Alps, caves, and coast within an hour's drive.

name: Ninh Binh
sample_activities: Take a sampan boat ride through the caves and karst scenery of Tam Coc
Cycle through rice paddies with limestone karsts towering above
Climb the 500 steps of Mua Cave for jaw-dropping panoramic views
Explore the ancient capital of Hoa Lu and its 10th-century temples
Boat through the vast wetlands of Van Long Nature Reserve spotting langurs
highlights: Ninh Binh is Vietnam's answer to Ha Long Bay but on land — towering karst formations rising from emerald rice paddies, with sampan boats gliding through river caves. It's only two hours from Hanoi but feels like a completely different world, and a fraction of the tourists make it here.

name: Pondicherry
sample_activities: Walk the pastel French Quarter streets and sit on the promenade at sunrise
Visit Auroville — the experimental international township in the forest
Eat French-Tamil fusion cuisine at a courtyard restaurant in the White Town
Take a yoga or meditation session at the Sri Aurobindo Ashram
Cycle to the mangrove-lined backwaters of the Chunnambar Boat House
highlights: Pondicherry is where France and Tamil Nadu unexpectedly collide — bougainvillea-draped colonial streets giving way to Hindu temples, with croissants and dosas served side by side. The nearby experimental community of Auroville adds a genuinely unique spiritual dimension found nowhere else in India.

name: Takayama
sample_activities: Walk the beautifully preserved Sanmachi Suji old merchant district
Try Hida beef — Japan's lesser-known rival to Kobe — grilled on a magnolia leaf
Browse the traditional morning markets for pickles, crafts, and mochi
Stay in a ryokan with tatami mats, kaiseki dinner, and private onsen
Visit the thatched-roof farmhouses of the nearby Shirakawa-go UNESCO village
highlights: Takayama is the Japan of your imagination — wooden merchant houses, sake breweries marked by cedar balls, morning markets, and mountain scenery that turns crimson in autumn. It's the kind of place where a ryokan owner still greets you at the door and draws your bath by hand.

name: Wadi Rum
sample_activities: Sleep in a Bedouin desert camp under one of the clearest night skies on Earth
Take a 4x4 jeep tour through red sandstone canyons and natural rock bridges
Climb Jebel Um Ishrin for sunset views across the rust-red desert
Eat zarb — Bedouin meat and vegetables cooked underground in sand
Scramble through Khazali Canyon to see 2,000-year-old Nabataean inscriptions
highlights: Wadi Rum is the desert that played Mars in Hollywood but feels even more otherworldly in person — towering sandstone monoliths rising from red sand, with Bedouin camps offering tea, zarb feasts, and night skies thick with stars. Lawrence of Arabia called it 'vast, echoing, and godlike' and he wasn't exaggerating.

name: Chefchaouen
sample_activities: Wander the blue-washed medina streets at golden hour for spectacular photography
Hike to the Spanish Mosque on the hillside for panoramic views of the blue city
Shop for hand-woven Rif Mountain blankets and goat cheese in the medina
Swim in the natural pools at Akchour Waterfalls in the surrounding Rif Mountains
Sip fresh mint tea on a rooftop terrace overlooking the blue-painted kasbah
highlights: Chefchaouen is Morocco's blue pearl — a medina painted in every shade of blue imaginable, tucked into the Rif Mountains with a pace of life that's worlds away from the hustle of Marrakech or Fez. It's the most photogenic town in North Africa and one of the most relaxed.

name: Lake Bled
sample_activities: Row a traditional pletna boat to the island church and ring the wishing bell
Hike to the clifftop Bled Castle for the best lake panorama
Eat the legendary kremsnita (cream cake) at the Park Hotel cafe
Walk the Vintgar Gorge on wooden walkways above emerald water
Swim in the warm lake from the public beach on the north shore
highlights: Lake Bled is almost absurdly picturesque — an emerald lake with a tiny island church, a cliff-hanging medieval castle, and the Julian Alps as a backdrop. It's small enough to walk around in two hours, but the surrounding gorges, valleys, and alpine trails could fill a week.

name: Siargao Island
sample_activities: Surf the famous Cloud 9 barrel wave or take lessons at a beginner break
Island-hop to the pristine Naked Island, Daku Island, and Guyam Island
Swim in the Sugba Lagoon's turquoise waters surrounded by limestone cliffs
Ride a motorbike through palm tree-lined roads to Magpupungko rock pools
Eat fresh seafood at a beachside warung watching the sunset
highlights: Siargao is the Philippines' surfing mecca, but its appeal goes way beyond waves — palm-fringed lagoons, mangrove forests, and tiny islands with white-sand beaches that would cost a fortune anywhere else. It has the barefoot, community vibe that Bali had decades ago, with none of the overdevelopment.

name: Antigua Guatemala
sample_activities: Watch sunrise from the summit of Volcan Acatenango with Fuego erupting beside you
Take a single-origin coffee tour from bean to cup at a local finca
Study Spanish at one of the many immersive language schools
Wander the cobblestoned streets past earthquake-ruined convents draped in bougainvillea
Shop for hand-woven huipil textiles at the Nim Po't cooperative
highlights: Antigua is a colonial jewel set between three volcanoes — cobblestoned streets lined with crumbling convents, vibrant textiles markets, and some of the world's finest single-origin coffee growing on the slopes above town. It's one of Central America's safest and most walkable cities, with an active volcano you can watch erupt from the neighbouring peak.

name: Udaipur
sample_activities: Watch sunset from a rooftop restaurant overlooking the floating Lake Palace
Take a boat ride on Lake Pichola past the City Palace's marble facades
Wander the old city's narrow lanes of miniature painting workshops and silver jewellers
Attend a Rajasthani folk music and dance performance at Bagore Ki Haveli
Day-trip to the exquisite Jain temples at Ranakpur carved from white marble
highlights: Udaipur is the Venice of the East — white marble palaces floating on still lakes, surrounded by the Aravalli Hills, with a romantic atmosphere that's earned it a place in Bond films and royal wedding dreams. Behind the palace grandeur, the old city bazaars are a maze of miniature painters, silversmiths, and spice sellers.

name: Meteora
sample_activities: Visit the six active monasteries perched on top of towering sandstone pillars
Hike between the monasteries on footpaths through the rock forest
Watch sunset from the viewpoint near the Great Meteoron monastery
Rock climb the sandstone towers with a local guide — where monks once ascended by rope
Explore the caves at the base of the pillars where hermit monks lived
highlights: Meteora is one of those places that looks photoshopped but isn't — six active Orthodox monasteries perched on top of sandstone pillars that rise 300 metres from the Thessalian plain. It's Greece beyond the islands, and the combination of natural grandeur and spiritual atmosphere is genuinely unique in Europe.

name: San Cristobal de las Casas
sample_activities: Visit the Tzotzil Maya village of San Juan Chamula and its extraordinary syncretic church
Browse the amber museum and indigenous textile cooperatives
Sip single-origin Chiapas coffee at one of the town's artisan roasters
Explore the colourful pedestrianised streets and street food markets
Hike to the Sumidero Canyon for views from 1,000-metre cliff edges
highlights: San Cristobal is a highland colonial town that serves as the gateway to the living Maya world of Chiapas — indigenous communities that practice syncretic rituals merging Catholicism with ancient beliefs in ways found nowhere else. At 2,200m, the cool pine-scented air and fireplace-warmed cafes are a world away from Mexico's beach resorts.

name: Galle
sample_activities: Walk the ramparts of the UNESCO-listed Dutch Fort at sunset
Browse the boutique shops and cafes tucked into colonial-era buildings
Watch cricket on the fort's pitch — one of the world's most scenic grounds
Visit a sea turtle hatchery along the southern coast
Eat hoppers and fresh fish curry at a family-run cafe in the fort
highlights: Galle Fort is a walled colonial time capsule on Sri Lanka's southern tip — Dutch-era ramparts enclosing a grid of streets now filled with boutique guesthouses, art galleries, and some of the island's best restaurants. Watching the sunset from the lighthouse rampart with fishermen casting below is an everyday pleasure here.

name: Bergen
sample_activities: Take the Floibanen funicular to Mount Floyen for views over the city and fjords
Wander the colourful wooden Bryggen wharf — a UNESCO World Heritage trading post
Eat freshly caught king crab and fish cakes at the Fish Market
Day-trip on the Norway in a Nutshell route through Sognefjord
Hike to the top of Ulriken — the highest of Bergen's seven mountains
highlights: Bergen is the gateway to Norway's fjords and one of Europe's most charming cities — a cluster of colourful wooden houses crammed between seven mountains and a working harbour. It rains a lot, but locals embrace it with an umbrella in one hand and a coffee in the other.

name: Nong Khiaw
sample_activities: Kayak the Nam Ou River through limestone karst gorges
Hike to the Pha Tok Caves used as shelters during the Secret War
Watch sunset from the Nong Khiaw Viewpoint after a steep jungle climb
Take a boat upriver to the remote village of Muang Ngoi
Sleep in a riverside bamboo bungalow and wake to misty mountain views
highlights: Nong Khiaw is a tiny river village wedged between soaring limestone cliffs in northern Laos — the kind of place where electricity arrived recently and the only traffic is longboat engines on the Nam Ou. It's one of Southeast Asia's last truly peaceful escapes, best reached by slow boat from Luang Prabang.

name: Sapa
sample_activities: Trek through cascading rice terraces to homestay with Hmong or Dao families
Visit the Bac Ha Sunday market where hill tribes trade livestock and crafts
Conquer Fansipan — Indochina's highest peak — by trekking or cable car
Learn indigo dyeing techniques from Black Hmong women in Cat Cat village
Soak in natural hot springs in the Muong Hoa Valley
highlights: Sapa is where Vietnam's rugged northern mountains meet centuries-old hill-tribe culture — terraced rice paddies carved into mountainsides by Hmong, Dao, and Tay communities who still wear traditional clothing and farm by hand. The trekking is spectacular, but the homestay experience with local families is what stays with you.

name: Porto Santo Island
sample_activities: Spend a day on the 9km golden sand beach — one of Europe's finest and least crowded
Visit Columbus's house where he lived before discovering the Americas
Hike the volcanic peaks at the eastern end of the island
Try the local therapeutic sand baths believed to ease joint pain
Eat fresh espada fish and drink local wine at a seaside tasca
highlights: Porto Santo is Madeira's forgotten little sister — a tiny island with a 9-kilometre golden beach that would be world-famous if it were anywhere warmer. It sees a fraction of Madeira's visitors, meaning you can have a European beach largely to yourself even in July.

name: AlUla
sample_activities: Explore Hegra — Saudi Arabia's first UNESCO site with 111 Nabataean tombs
Take a helicopter flight over the sandstone rock formations of the desert canyon
Stargaze from a luxury desert camp in the Ashar Valley
Wander through the abandoned mud-brick old town of AlUla
Visit Elephant Rock and picnic beneath its wind-carved form at sunset
highlights: AlUla is Saudi Arabia's answer to Petra — a vast desert canyon hiding 7,000 years of civilisation, from Nabataean tombs to Dadanite inscriptions, only opened to international tourists in 2020. It's one of the world's last major archaeological sites to be revealed, and the landscape of sandstone monoliths is breathtaking.

name: Faroe Islands
sample_activities: Hike to the optical illusion lake Sorvagsvatn that appears to hover above the ocean
Visit the puffin colonies on Mykines island during nesting season
Drive through the subsea tunnel between islands — the world's first undersea roundabout
Explore the grass-roofed village of Gasadalur with its cliffside waterfall
Eat fermented lamb and wind-dried fish at a traditional Faroese restaurant
highlights: The Faroe Islands are 18 volcanic islands in the North Atlantic where grass-roofed villages cling to cliffs, sheep outnumber people, and the weather changes five times an hour. It's Iceland's quieter, more intimate sibling — raw, windswept, and utterly spectacular for hikers and photographers.

name: Gijon
sample_activities: Learn to pour Asturian cider the traditional way — held high above the glass
Walk the San Lorenzo beach promenade and swim in the Bay of Biscay
Eat fabada asturiana and fresh seafood at a sidreria in Cimadevilla
Explore the Roman baths beneath the old fishing quarter
Day-trip to the dramatic Picos de Europa mountains for hiking
highlights: Gijon is Spain's best-kept secret — an Atlantic port city in Asturias where the cider flows like water, the seafood is impeccable, and international tourists are almost nonexistent. Green Spain is the anti-Costa del Sol, offering surf beaches, mountain scenery, and a fiercely proud regional food culture.

name: Ipoh
sample_activities: Eat the famous Ipoh white coffee and bean sprout chicken at a traditional kopitiam
Explore the Sam Poh Tong cave temple with its limestone stalactite formations
Walk the Concubine Lane heritage area for street food and vintage shops
Visit the Kellie's Castle — an unfinished colonial mansion in the jungle
Take a street art walk through the old town's colonial-era streets
highlights: Ipoh is Malaysia's most underrated food city — a colonial tin-mining town where Cantonese, Malay, and Indian cuisines produce dishes you won't find anywhere else, from silky hor fun noodles to egg tarts. The limestone cave temples surrounding the city add a genuinely dramatic natural backdrop.

name: Kazbegi (Stepantsminda)
sample_activities: Hike to the Gergeti Trinity Church perched at 2,170m with Mount Kazbek behind it
Drive the Georgian Military Highway through the Jvari Pass
Attempt the two-day summit of Mount Kazbek at 5,054m with a guide
Stay in a guesthouse and eat homemade khinkali and churchkhela with the family
Soak in natural hot springs in the Truso Valley
highlights: Kazbegi is the Caucasus at its most magnificent — a tiny mountain town dominated by the 5,054m cone of Mount Kazbek, with a medieval church floating above the clouds on its slopes. The Georgian Military Highway drive to get here is one of the world's great road trips, and the hospitality once you arrive is overwhelming.
